matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: number system: introduction and classification grade 9 students number system overview classification of numbers decimal expansions real number line the number system: a journey of classification introduction to number systems subject: mathematics - class 9 chapter: number system (session 1) essential question: are all numbers rational? theme: from counting to infinity what we will learn today learning objectives classify numbers into natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational. identify terminating vs. non-terminating repeating decimals. locate rational numbers on the number line using successive magnification. building on prior knowledge natural numbers whole numbers natural numbers (n): counting numbers {1, 2, 3...} whole numbers (w): natural numbers + zero {0, 1, 2...} integers (z): whole numbers and their negatives {...-2, -1, 0, 1, 2...} can every fraction be written as a decimal? rational vs irrational we know 1/2 = 0.5 (terminating) we know 1/3 = 0.33... (repeating) what about the square root of 2? is it a fraction? is it rational? the two families of real numbers rational numbers definition irrational numbers definition rational numbers: can be expressed as p/q where...
